rotation: The act of using a QR code to withdraw money from a shared credit card limit — tracked as a card_transaction with type='withdrawal' and status='pending_settlement'.
settlement: Closing a pending rotation by confirming the cash receiver — updates card_transaction status to 'settled' and adjusts cash_on_hand balances.
bill pay: Repaying the credit card bill — inserts a card_transaction with type='bill_payment' and updates the billing_cycle paid_amount.
spend: A personal expense recorded against a card limit or cash balance — stored in the 'spends' table with spend_type='personal'.
lent: A personal loan given to someone outside the group — tracked in the 'lents' table with borrower_name, amount, due_date, and payment_history.
vault: The collection of QR codes available for rotation — displayed in the 'QR Vault' tab in settlements/qr.tsx.
cooling period: 24-hour lockout after a QR code is used — enforced by checking last_used_date on the qr record; the qr_cooling_period_alert is sent 24h5m later via QStash.
in transit: Rotation transactions with status='pending_settlement' — shown in the 'In Transit' tab of the settlements page.
primary card: A credit card with is_primary=true — holds the total limit and has its own billing cycles. Sub-cards share its limit.
sub-card: A credit card with is_primary=false and a parent_card_id — shares the primary card's limit. Created for additional card holders.
family cards: All cards in the same primary card group — the primary itself plus all sub-cards with matching parent_card_id.
family limit: The aggregated available limit for a primary card group — stored in familyLimitsMap in transactions/page.tsx, derived from total_limit minus rotations and bill payments.
cash on hand: Per-user per-card cash balance — stored in the cash_on_hand table, debited on spend/bill payment and credited on settlement.
billing cycle: A monthly credit card billing period — tracked in billing_cycles table with generated_amount, paid_amount, and status (unpaid/partially_paid/paid).
manual entry: A settlement created without a prior QR rotation — entered via the 'Manual Entry' button in settlements/page.tsx handleManualEntry function.
acting user: The team member on whose behalf a transaction is recorded — selected in the transaction modal's user dropdown, stored as finalActingUserId in handleSave.
card access: The permission mapping between users and cards — stored in card_access table (user_id + card_id).
global card filter: The header dropdown in transactions and settlements pages that filters the ledger by card — stored in globalSelectedCardIds in cardStore.
QR mark used: The action of clicking a QR to start a rotation — calls markQrAsUsedToday in settlements/qr.tsx which updates last_used_date and triggers WhatsApp alerts.
components array: The Meta Cloud API format for WhatsApp template parameters — an array of objects with type ('header'/'body') and parameters with parameter_name keys. Required for templates with header variables.
fire and forget: Non-blocking WhatsApp alert pattern — fetch called without await and .catch logs error. Used in transactions/page.tsx sendAlerts() to not block the UI.
QStash: Upstash message queue service used to schedule WhatsApp cooling alerts — delivered via Upstash-Delay header on POST to /api/send-whatsapp.
proxy: The Next.js 16 auth middleware file — must be named proxy.ts with a named export called 'proxy' (not middleware.ts with default export, which is Next.js 13-15 pattern).
sanitizeText: Helper function that normalizes Unicode spaces, removes invisible characters, and trims text before sending in WhatsApp template parameters.
triggerAlert: Fire-and-forget WhatsApp helper in transactions/page.tsx — wraps fetch to /api/send-whatsapp with .catch, no await.
sendAlerts: Closure defined inside handleSave in transactions/page.tsx — captures transaction context and fires all relevant WhatsApp alerts based on txType (bill/rotate/spend).
debt repayment: A special spend type (spend_type='repayment', amount negative) recorded when a user clears a personal debt — toggled via isDebtRepayment in the bill pay modal.
split payment: When a transaction amount exceeds either credit limit or cash balance — automatically split between credit_card and cash_on_hand sources, tracked as two separate spend/transaction rows.
